title: Will rain finally bring relief from heat? Four-day weather alert across Karnataka - NewsFirst Prime
full_text:
IMD issues yellow alerts for multiple districts till April 23; rain, thunderstorms and hail expected even as heatwave conditions continue in coastal regions.
Karnataka is likely to witness a mix of rain and rising temperatures over the next four days, according to the India Meteorological Department. The weather department has issued alerts across several districts, warning of rainfall, thunderstorms, hailstorms and even heatwave conditions in some regions.
On Day 1, rain activity is expected in nearly half of the state. Districts including Bidar, Kalaburagi, Vijayapura, Bagalkot, Belagavi, Dharwad and Gadag in north Karnataka are under alert. In addition, Uttara Kannada, Shivamogga, Udupi, Chikkamagaluru, Hassan, Kodagu and Dakshina Kannada are also likely to receive rainfall. Some of these areas may witness thunderstorms accompanied by hail.
Despite rain warnings, daytime temperatures are expected to rise significantly. Coastal districts such as Uttara Kannada, Udupi and Dakshina Kannada have been placed under a heatwave alert, indicating continued discomfort due to high temperatures.
On April 21, the alert expands to 16 districts. Apart from Bidar, Kalaburagi and Vijayapura, districts like Yadgir, Raichur, Koppal, Ballari, Vijayanagara, Davanagere, Chitradurga, Tumakuru, Mysuru and Chamarajanagar are also expected to see thunderstorm activity. Coastal districts including Uttara Kannada, Udupi and Dakshina Kannada remain under alert.
On April 22, as many as 17 districts are expected to receive rainfall. The list includes Kalaburagi, Vijayapura, Yadgir, Raichur, Koppal, Gadag, Ballari, Vijayanagara, Haveri, Davanagere, Uttara Kannada, Belagavi, Udupi, Dakshina Kannada, Hassan, Kodagu and Chamarajanagar. Thunderstorms are likely in several of these areas.
By April 23, the number of districts under alert reduces to 10. Belagavi, Gadag, Haveri, Shivamogga, Chikkamagaluru, Hassan, Udupi, Dakshina Kannada, Uttara Kannada and Kodagu are expected to receive rain with thunder.
For the rest of the state, including Bengaluru, light to moderate rainfall is expected along with relatively stable temperatures.
The IMD has advised people to stay alert, especially in areas expecting thunderstorms, hail and heatwave conditions, as the state experiences fluctuating weather patterns over the coming days.
